# migration_name: # phase must be expand, contract, or metadata-only.
# previous_expand_release is required for destructive contract migrations; use n/a otherwise.
# old_code_compatible must be yes for any migration with breaking SQL matched by the deploy guard.

lock_impact_plan: Adds PaymentTransaction plus indexes/FK references to Payment; run during the normal deploy window, verify no long payment transactions before migrate, and let the deploy guard stop on lock timeout/failure.

Drops and recreates FK constraints on Booking, Payment, MemberCredit, RefundRequest, and related finance tables without removing columns; run during low traffic, check for long write transactions first, and roll back before traffic cutover if migrate fails.

Adds nullable/default PaymentRefund ledger columns, reads Payment and PaymentTransaction for a bounded backfill, then creates indexes; run during low traffic and verify payment writes are quiet before migrate.

Backfills NominationToken.tokenHash from existing plaintext tokens, recreates unique lookup coverage on tokenHash, then drops the plaintext token column; run during low traffic and verify no active nomination confirmations are in flight before migrate.

Adds nullable Member columns (profileCompletedAt, detailsConfirmedAt, detailsConfirmedByMemberId, onboardingConfirmedAt), a self-referential nullable FK with ON DELETE SET NULL, and a supporting index. Existing rows are not backfilled; old code ignores the new columns and new code sets them on confirmation. ADD COLUMN with no default takes a brief AccessExclusive lock on Member; run during low traffic.

Adds six nullable FamilyGroupJoinRequest columns (requestedFirstName/LastName/DateOfBirth/Email, subjectMemberId, requestNotes), two indexes, and a nullable FK to Member with ON DELETE SET NULL. Old code ignores the new columns; FamilyGroupJoinRequest is low-write so lock impact is minimal.

Backfills legacy Booking rows from CONFIRMED to PAYMENT_PENDING only when no SUCCEEDED Payment exists. This is run after the PAYMENT_PENDING enum migration and after production app containers include a Prisma client that can read the enum. The current affected set is one row; run during low traffic and verify the count before and after migrate.

Backfills legacy Booking rows from CONFIRMED to PAID when a SUCCEEDED Payment exists. Old code already understands PAID bookings, and new code depends on the repaired status for capacity and lodge surfaces. Run during low traffic and verify CONFIRMED bookings grouped by payment status before and after migrate.

Adds nullable IssueReport triage and sensitive-data retention columns plus indexes, then backfills 30-day screenshot/browser-info expiry timestamps for existing rows. Old code ignores the new columns; run during low traffic because IssueReport is low-write and the backfill is bounded by recent support reports.
Creates email message setting, template override, and notification delivery policy tables plus indexes. Old code ignores the new tables; locks are limited to new objects.

Creates payment recovery enums/table/indexes/FKs and backfills risky zero-dollar pending PaymentIntent rows from Booking, Payment, and PaymentTransaction. Old code ignores the queue; run during low traffic and verify pending payment transaction counts before migrate.

Adds BookingGuest stay range columns, backfills from Booking, installs a trigger so old app inserts derive stayStart/stayEnd from the parent booking, then enforces NOT NULL and creates range indexes. BookingGuest is write-active, so run during low traffic, verify no NULL ranges after migrate, and let the deploy guard stop on lock timeout/failure.

Adds nullable Member cancellation columns plus membership cancellation request/settings tables, indexes, and FKs. Old code ignores the new columns and tables; run during low traffic because Member and cancellation workflows are low-write.

Adds participant confirmation enum values plus nullable hashed-token and timestamp columns to MembershipCancellationRequestParticipant. Old code ignores the new columns; run during low traffic because membership cancellation requests are low-write, and no existing rows are backfilled.

Adds MemberLifecycleAction and MemberLifecycleActionRequestStatus enums plus the MemberLifecycleActionRequest table for second-admin delete approvals. Old code ignores the new table; run during low traffic because member lifecycle requests are low-write, and no existing rows are backfilled.
Creates BookingChangeRequest enum/table/indexes/FKs for member edit review. Old code ignores the new queue; run during low traffic and rely on the status/createdAt indexes for admin queue reads.

Adds the ARCHIVE lifecycle enum value and nullable Member archive metadata with a SET NULL FK to MemberLifecycleActionRequest. Old code ignores the new enum value and columns; run during low traffic because Member writes are low volume.

Adds a CHECK constraint enforcing stayStart < stayEnd on BookingGuest. Existing rows were backfilled by 20260524090000_add_booking_guest_stay_ranges; before migrate verify no invalid BookingGuest ranges remain and run during low traffic because BookingGuest writes are capacity-sensitive.
Adds a default-true NotificationPreference flag for admin booking-change-request alerts. Existing admin notification rows backfill through the default value; run during normal low traffic.
Adds a PaymentRecoveryOperationType enum value for booking-modification refunds. Old code ignores the new value; deploy before any runtime enqueues the new operation type.

Renames BookingChangeRequest status values and Member FK columns to match sibling review queues. Treat as a controlled low-traffic migration from the prior booking-change runtime: verify the booking-change request queue is idle, ensure old app colors are drained or blocked from booking-change request traffic, and use the breaking-migration override only with that operator acknowledgement.

Adds nullable BookingChangeRequest.linkedModificationId plus an index and SET NULL FK to BookingModification. Old code ignores the nullable column; run during low traffic because BookingModification is a booking-write table.

Adds triggers enforcing BookingGuest stay ranges inside the parent Booking envelope. Application code already writes envelope-bounded ranges; before migrate verify no existing violations and run during low traffic because Booking and BookingGuest writes are capacity-sensitive.

Renames promo-code cap columns, backfills maxUsesPerMember from singleUse, drops singleUse, and adds nullable/default cap fields. Treat as a controlled promo maintenance migration: verify promo administration and booking promo validation traffic is idle or routed to the new runtime only, and use the breaking-migration override only with that operator acknowledgement.

Creates PromoRedemptionAllocation, backfills one allocation per existing PromoRedemption, recalculates PromoCode.currentRedemptions from allocations, and installs PromoRedemption insert/update triggers so old app colors still create one-booker allocations during blue/green drain. Run during low promo-booking traffic because PromoRedemption and PromoCode are booking-write tables.
